proof_sketch: The mutation changes blockDim.x to blockDim.y in the half-precision kernel specialization (template<> for half). The launch configuration is 1D: <<<num_blocks, block_size>>> with blockDim.x=256, blockDim.y=1. For half inputs, the mutated index becomes blockIdx.x + threadIdx.x, which is fundamentally different from the original blockIdx.x * 256 + threadIdx.x. However, the fixed contract specifies input dtype=torch.float32, so the half kernel is never invoked. All tests under the main contract use float32 tensors, meaning the mutated code path is not executed. The half kernel is only triggered when the input dtype is half (float16), which is not part of the fixed input specification.
content:

  "reason_category": "path_not_triggered",
  "proof_sketch": "The mutation changes blockDim.x to blockDim.y in the half-precision kernel specialization (template<> for half). The launch configuration is 1D: <<<num_blocks, block_size>>> with blockDim.x=256, blockDim.y=1. For half inputs, the mutated index becomes blockIdx.x + threadIdx.x, which is fundamentally different from the original blockIdx.x * 256 + threadIdx.x. However, the fixed contract specifies input dtype=torch.float32, so the half kernel is never invoked. All tests under the main contract use float32 tensors, meaning the mutated code path is not executed. The half kernel is only triggered when the input dtype is half (float16), which is not part of the fixed input specification.",
  "survival_reason": "The mutation is located in the half-precision template specialization of the CUDA kernel. Under the fixed contract, the input tensor is always of dtype=torch.float32. The dispatch macro AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF will select the float32 kernel (which is unchanged) and never call the half kernel. Therefore, the mutated code is never reachable in any test that respects the contract. All 112 EMD rounds, 75 deterministic stress rounds, and auxiliary config-stress tests either used float32 inputs or, in the case of the separate dtype stress test, the half kernel may have been exercised but the results still survived; however, that test is outside the fixed contract. Under the given contract, the mutant is unkillable because the affected code path is never triggered.",
  "killable": false,
  "kill_strategy": "The mutant is unkillable under the fixed contract (float32 inputs). To kill it, one would need to change the input dtype to float16 or bfloat16, which is outside the contract. Since the contract prohibits shape/batch changes, a dtype change is not allowed. Thus, no value-level strategy within the contract can expose the difference.",
  "suggested_test": null,
  "recommendations": "To improve test coverage, the mutation testing framework should consider multi-dtype contracts. Include float16 and bfloat16 as part of the fixed input specification to catch mutations in template specializations. Additionally, verify that all kernel specializations are exercised in the main test suite, especially when the contract specifies only one dtype."
